steps.title: Find the key contrast
steps.content: The passage shows that stations near car rental companies (full‑price charging) get more negative reviews, while stations near restaurants/comfort stores (subsidized or free charging) get fewer negative reviews, with service assumed equal.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D states that users who receive a discount have lower expectations of quality than those who pay full price, which directly explains why subsidized stations earn fewer negative reviews.

The other three choices either ignore the price‑expectation link, introduce unrelated ideas about awareness or visit frequency, or reverse the relationship.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
